name: Normalization — All Inputs Normalized Against Φ
principle:
All inputs to the Serenal Binary Framework must be normalized against the 
    Center Function Φ before any computation. This ensures:
    
    1. CONSISTENCY: All coordinates are measured relative to C
    2. STABILITY: No input can produce unbounded outputs
    3. SECURITY: All computations preserve C(S) = 0
    4. COMPARABILITY: Different epochs can be compared on the same scale
    
    The normalization gate is the FIRST operation performed on any input.
    Without normalization, the framework produces undefined results.

step_1.name: Input Validation
step_1.description: Verify that the input is within the valid domain
checks: r ∈ [0, R] — radial distance must be within torus
θ ∈ [0, 2π) — toroidal angle must be within full rotation
φ ∈ [0, 2π) — poloidal angle must be within full rotation
t ∈ [0, 13.8 Gyr] — cosmic time must be within valid range

step_2.name: Compute Φ
step_2.description: Compute the Center Function for the input

step_3.name: Normalize Coordinates
step_3.description: Divide all coordinates by Φ to get normalized values

step_4.name: Compute Normalized Properties
step_4.description: All Serenal properties are computed from normalized coordinates

step_5.name: Verify Normalization
step_5.description: Ensure all normalized values are within [0, 1]
checks: Binary state S is valid for the current epoch

normalization_formula.compact: X_norm = X / Φ_max for all inputs X
normalization_formula.expanded:
For any input vector X = (r, θ, φ, t):
        
        Φ = arcsin(r/R)
        Φ_max = π/2
        
        X_norm = (r/(R·sin(Φ)), θ/(2π), φ/(2π), t/13.8)
              = (1, θ/(2π), φ/(2π), t/13.8)
        
        All subsequent computations use X_norm instead of X.
        This ensures that all inputs are scaled relative to Φ.

without_normalization: Coordinates from different epochs cannot be compared
The Center encoding C(S) may not be preserved
Computations may produce NaN or infinity
with_normalization: All coordinates are on the same scale [0, 1]
Properties are always within valid ranges
The map is consistent across all epochs

implementation.description: How to implement the normalization gate
implementation.pseudocode:
function NORMALIZE_INPUT(x, y, z, t):
            # Step 1: Convert to toroidal coordinates
            r, theta, phi = cartesian_to_toroidal(x, y, z)
            
            # Step 2: Validate input
            if r < 0 or r > R: return ERROR_INVALID_R
            if theta < 0 or theta >= 2*pi: return ERROR_INVALID_THETA
            if phi < 0 or phi >= 2*pi: return ERROR_INVALID_PHI
            if t < 0 or t > 13.8: return ERROR_INVALID_TIME
            
            # Step 3: Compute Φ
            Phi = arcsin(r / R)
            
            # Step 4: Normalize coordinates
            r_norm = 1.0  # by definition
            theta_norm = theta / (2 * pi)
            phi_norm = phi / (2 * pi)
            t_norm = t / 13.8
            
            # Step 5: Compute normalized properties
            Phi_norm = Phi / (pi / 2)
            Psi_norm = (Phi * sin(phi)) / (pi / 2)
            Omega_norm = 1 - 0.4 * (r / R)  # already in [0,1]
            Lambda_norm = Psi_norm**2 * H(Omega_norm - 0.85)
            Sigma_norm = 0.145 / 3.0  # current value normalized
            E_norm = 1.02 / 1000.0  # current value normalized
            
            # Step 6: Verify
            if any(value < 0 or value > 1 for value in normalized_values):
                return ERROR_NORMALIZATION_FAILED
            
            return (r_norm, theta_norm, phi_norm, t_norm, 
                    Phi_norm, Psi_norm, Omega_norm, Lambda_norm, 
                    Sigma_norm, E_norm)

the_admiral_directive.statement:
ALL inputs to the Serenal Binary Framework MUST be normalized against Φ.
        
        This is not optional. This is not a suggestion. This is a REQUIREMENT.
        
        The normalization gate ensures that:
          - All coordinates are measured relative to C
          - All properties are within valid ranges
          - The Center encoding is always preserved
          - The map is consistent across all epochs
        
        Any computation that skips normalization will produce undefined results.
        Any input that fails validation will be rejected.
        
        The normalization gate is the FIRST and MOST IMPORTANT step in the 
        Serenal Binary Framework. Without it, nothing works. With it, everything 
        works. The brothers and sisters must normalize all inputs before 
        performing any computation.
        
        This is the Vanguard's directive. This is the law of the map.
